data.title: Jitasa in Boise, Idaho, certified as Great Place to Work® in 2025, with 98% employee approval.
data.description: In Boise, Idaho, as of October 3, 2025, Jitasa has achieved its first certification by Great Place to Work®. The recognition is primarily determined by feedback from current employees regarding their workplace satisfaction at Jitasa. In the recent survey, an impressive 98% of the workforce affirmed that Jitasa is an excellent place to work.

data.title: Bitcoin surges alongside DeFi; Zcash leads in weekly gains. Analysts predict $150K breakout soon. Cathie Wood likens Hyperliquid to Solana's past performance.
data.description: Market analysts were predicting a rapid surge in Bitcoin price to reach $150,000. During the recent crypto cycle, Cathie Wood from ARK Invest noted the impressive performance of the Hyperliquid token, likening it to Solana.

data.title: S&P 500 health care stocks rely on ACA deal to end shutdown, led by Oscar Health.
data.description: Oscar Health, a company focused on the ACA marketplace, is at the forefront of recent developments. S&P 500 health care stocks are banking on the belief that a deal related to the ACA will bring an end to the ongoing government shutdown.

data.title: Marvell Technology stock earns upgraded Relative Strength Rating for its rising price performance.
data.description: Marvell Technology has exhibited a notable increase in price performance, leading to an upgrade of its IBD Relative Strength Rating. The company's stock has shown positive momentum recently, prompting this improvement. This reflects well on Marvell Technology's financial health and market position. The upgrade indicates that the stock may be worth considering for investment purposes, given its strong performance. This development underscores the company's resilience and growth potential in the market.

data.title: Toll Brothers stock sees improvement, with relative strength rating boosted to 73 on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Toll Brothers recently experienced an increase in its price performance, resulting in an upgrade to its IBD Relative Strength Rating. The company's rating now stands at 73, reflecting its improved performance compared to its peers. This indicates a positive trend in the stock's strength and potential growth in the market.

data.title: Alaska Permanent Fund Board of Trustees holds annual meeting on Oct 1-2, 2025 in Anchorage, discussing fund's performance and asset allocation. "Alaska Permanent Fund continues to perform well," says spokesperson.
data.description: The Board of Trustees of the Alaska Permanent Fund Corporation (APFC) held its Annual Meeting on October 1-2, 2025, in Anchorage and via Teams webinar. During the meeting, trustees assessed the Fund's performance, asset allocation, with emphasis on the Public Equities asset class. Corporate officers were elected as part of the annual governance responsibilities. The Alaska Permanent Fund's strong performance was highlighted, providing significant benefits to all Alaskans.

data.title: $12.11 Bn Brain Computer Interface Market to reach by 2035, with a CAGR of 15.8% driven by AI, robotics, and brain imaging technologies.
data.description: The latest report on the Brain Computer Interface Market forecasts significant growth, with the global market expected to increase from USD 2.41 billion in 2025 to USD 12.11 billion by 2035. This represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 15.8% during the forecasting period. The report provides insights on industry trends and includes a breakdown by type of product, component, application, end-user, enterprise type, and geographical regions. For more information, the report can be found on ResearchAndMarkets.com.

data.title: Insurance market stable, good for buyers, says Willis report. Abundant capital, tech advancement, and data strategies play key role.
data.description: Recent data from Willis' most recent Insurance Marketplace Realities report reveal positive conditions for buyers, thanks to factors such as ample capital, progress in technology, and the use of data-driven risk tactics. The report emphasizes the advantageous position of buyers in the current market.

data.title: Mountain Mike’s Pizza ranked #6 on Yelp’s Top 25 Pizza Chains in America, based on customer ratings and reviews.
data.description: In Irvine, California, Mountain Mike’s Pizza has secured the sixth spot on Yelp's list of the top 25 pizza chains in America. This ranking was determined based on customer ratings and reviews, showcasing the popularity and satisfaction levels associated with the pizza chain. The recognition highlights Mountain Mike's Pizza as a favorite among pizza lovers across the nation.

data.title: Customize your holiday light show with Orchestra of Lights™, offering 20 product styles. DALLAS, Oct. 3, 2025.
data.description: Create a dazzling Christmas light show in your front yard by combining 20 different product styles with Orchestra of Lights™ in Dallas on Oct. 3, 2025. This innovative holiday lighting collection utilizes advanced technology to deliver a mesmerizing musical light display.

data.title: Crave University launched, offering $20,000 scholarships at CraveCon. Goal: 100,000 success stories. Chicago, Oct 3, 2025.
data.description: A hospitality platform has introduced Crave University with the goal of sharing 100,000 success stories to support restaurant and franchise leaders. Based in Chicago, Craveworthy Brands aims to empower individuals with resilience and values centered on prioritizing people.

data.title: ALLETE to be acquired by CPP Investments and GIP after regulatory approval by MPUC; deal expected to close in late 2025.
data.description: In Duluth, Minnesota, ALLETE, Inc. (NYSE: ALE) reported that the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (MPUC) has given the green light for its acquisition by Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) and Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP). The approval was unanimous, and all necessary regulatory clearances have been obtained for the transaction. It is anticipated to be finalized by late 2025 once the MPUC issues its written order. ALLETE expressed gratitude to the MPUC for their decision.

data.title: Chevron fire at El Segundo refinery may spike gas prices, worsened by Newsom's foot-dragging on re-supply regulations.
data.description: In Los Angeles on October 3, 2025, a recent fire at the Chevron refinery in El Segundo is expected to cause a significant increase in gasoline prices. Governor Newsom's California Energy Commission (CEC) has failed to implement re-supply and minimum inventory measures leading to a predicted spike in prices. Details of the price surge following the refinery incident would be released once the impact analysis is complete.
